{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2025-43863", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 1.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:H/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:L/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:U"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-307", "name": "Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts"}], "description": "### Impact
If attacker gets access to an authenticated session, they can try to brute-force the user password by using the change password functionality: they can call that route infinitely which will return the message that password is wrong until it is correct

### Patches
This issue has been patched in version 4.11.0

### Workarounds
